postData.contentHtml:
In a truly remarkable discovery, the deep waters off Fiordland, New Zealand, have yielded an extraordinary find: a colossal 14-foot (4-meter) black coral. This ancient marine marvel is estimated to be approximately 400 years old, offering a rare glimpse into centuries of oceanic life.

The discovery has sent ripples of excitement through the scientific community. A seasoned marine biologist, with 25 years of experience under their belt, described the creature as an unprecedented find. "It's unlike anything he had ever encountered," and "by far the largest" they have ever seen, highlighting the sheer scale and uniqueness of this black coral.

This monumental organism not only stands as a testament to the longevity of deep-sea life but also promises to unlock a wealth of hidden ocean mysteries. Its age, predominantly reported as 400 years, with some mentions of 300 years, underscores the importance of continued exploration in these remote marine environments.

Initial reactions to the discovery across social media have been overwhelmingly positive, sparking widespread fascination and discussions about the wonders that still lie undiscovered beneath the waves. This find reinforces New Zealand's reputation as a hotspot for unique and ancient marine biodiversity, encouraging further research into its pristine, deep-sea ecosystems.
postData.title: A 400-Year-Old Giant Black Coral Discovered in New Zealand's Deep
postData.summary: A monumental 14-foot black coral, estimated to be up to 400 years old, has been found in the unexplored depths of Fiordland, New Zealand, captivating marine biologists and revealing hidden ocean mysteries.

sources.title: A 14-Foot Black Coral Hid in New Zealand’s Deep for 400 Years
“It’s by far the largest I’ve ever seen in my 25 years as a marine biologist” new 4-metre giant, 400-year-old creature discovered
A 300-year-old giant black coral was discovered in New Zealand’s deep seas, revealing hidden ocean mysteries
